name: Redirection Drill — Steering a Talkative Patient
_note: Skill drill. Standard 4 metric keys re-pitched: medical_accuracy = politeness/tact, clinical_reasoning slot = steering & structure (the focus), communication_fluency = graceful interjection.

anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; meaning always clear

anchors.9-10: Redirects warmly and respectfully; patient never feels dismissed
anchors.5-6: Gets the job done but a little abrupt
anchors.1-2: Rude or makes the patient feel shut down

anchors.9-10: Consistently refocuses on the main issue; clear signposting; good use of time
anchors.5-6: Some steering but lets the patient ramble often
anchors.1-2: No control of the conversation at all

anchors.9-10: Interrupts gracefully at natural moments without jarring the patient
anchors.1-2: Cannot interject; either silent or talks over the patient

anchors.9-10: Exceptional empathy and professionalism; creates psychological safety
anchors.7-8: Good empathy; acknowledges emotion; polite and professional
anchors.5-6: Adequate but mechanical empathy; some emotional cues missed
anchors.3-4: Little empathic response; information delivery prioritized
anchors.1-2: No empathic response; dismissive or unprofessional

checklist.item: politely redirected the patient back to the main issue
used signposting (e.g. 'Can we come back to your cough?')
acknowledged the tangent briefly before steering
kept the consultation focused on the main complaint
maintained warmth and rapport while steering
